title: port forwarding support for user supplied network namespace `--network ns:`
body:
### Feature request description

Currently, `podman run -d --name test --publish 8080:8080 --network ns:/run/netns/xxx docker.io/alpine
` runs the container in the given network namespace. The --port option is silently ignored and `podman inspect test -f {{.NetworkSettings.Ports}}` returns empty. I did not find any documentation stating that the 2 options cannot be used together. 

### Suggest potential solution

1. use rootlessport to forward the port like with slirp4netns
   - intuitive for users
   - implementation should be very similar to slirp4netns.SetupRootlessPortMappingViaRLK
   - need to guess/know what ip to connect to for the rkp client running in the container, loopback should be good enough for most containers
   - questionable security. not sure which uid owns the rootlessport process if podman is under uid 0, but the attack surface is small nonetheless.
 2. document this as not supported, and show an error when --publish and --network is used together

### Have you considered any alternatives?

_No response_

### Additional context

_No response_

comments.body:
Hey @jrl-oss 

Thank you for reporting this issue. TLDR: I do see room for improvement with this.

Using `--network ns:/run/netns/xxx` is an advanced feature, by passing this option you are effectively telling Podman you want to manually maintain the network namespace and will create your own port forwarding rules etc.

If you would like to have some control over the network namespace podman uses the supported path is to create the network using `podman network create` and launch containers with `--network {{ network name }}`. 

As you say this is currently undocumented and failing silently, let me open a PR to improve this and in the meantime maintainers with more network experience can chime in :)
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
